user_query:
Set up an event-triggered automation that watches the folder input/new_records. When any file in that folder is added or modified, immediately (1) parse the provided census and obituary sources to extract structured records, (2) filter and rank those records against my target surnames and counties, and (3) generate a reports bundle plus a ready-to-send newsletter email draft.

Inputs you must use:
- input/new_records/census_1920.csv (CSV). Columns: full_name, surname, given_name, county, state, birth_year, event_year, relation_hint, source_quality, source_id, url.
- input/new_records/obituaries.html (HTML). For each <div class="obit"> extract: full_name (text of .name), surname (last word of full_name), county (text of .county), state (text of .state), event_year (YYYY from .date), relation_hint (text of .relation_hint), source_quality (float from .source_quality), source_id (value of data-source-id), url (href of .url).
- input/targets.yaml (YAML). Use surnames and counties as filters, and weights to compute the relevance score. top_n defines how many records to include in the top list; newsletter_title defines the email subject.
- input/subscribers.csv (CSV). Columns: name, email. Use to populate the "To:" line in the email draft.

Processing requirements:
- Read and extract the relevant fields from all inputs.
- Case-insensitive filtering: keep a record if its surname is in targets.yaml.surnames OR its county is in targets.yaml.counties.
- Compute a relevance score for each kept record as:
  score = (surname_match ? weights.surname_match : 0)
        + (county_match ? weights.county_match : 0)
        + (relation_hint_bonus mapped from weights.relation_hint_bonus; missing keys count as 0)
        + (source_quality * weights.source_quality_multiplier).
- Sort kept records by score descending; tie-break by full_name ascending.
- Export the top targets.yaml.top_n records as the “top ranked” list.

Deliverables (regenerate on every trigger):
- output/reports/all_scored.csv: All kept records after filtering, one row per record. Required columns (in order): full_name, surname, county, state, event_type ("census" or "obituary"), event_year, relation_hint, source_quality, source_id, source ("census_1920" or "obituaries"), score.
- output/reports/top_ranked.csv: The top targets.yaml.top_n records with the same columns as above plus a rank column (1-based).
- output/drafts/email_to_subscribers.txt: A plain-text email draft with:
  - A To: line containing a comma-separated list of all emails from input/subscribers.csv.
  - A Subject: line using targets.yaml.newsletter_title.
  - A Body that begins with "Hi there, fellow researchers," followed by a concise summary and a bullet list of the top targets.yaml.top_n records. Each bullet must include: full_name, event_type, county, state, event_year, and the computed score rounded to one decimal.
- output/logs/run.log: Append a single line per run with an ISO timestamp and counts: total_records_parsed, kept_records_after_filter, top_n_exported.

Automation behavior:
- Watch input/new_records for file creations or modifications. On any such event, rebuild all outputs as specified. On initial execution, process the current files once immediately.

Notes:
- All matching and ranking are case-insensitive on text fields.
- Overwrite output/reports/*.csv and output/drafts/email_to_subscribers.txt on each run; append to output/logs/run.log.
- Do not use the Internet; all data is local.
- The outputs must be reproducible from the provided inputs and rules without manual steps. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
